request_summary: Valhalla V3 — real-time Norse Agent System pipeline monitoring dashboard. React 18 + TypeScript + Vite frontend, Express backend, filesystem data sources.

items.title: Filesystem data reader: scan portfolio projects and parse state.yaml + execution-log.json
items.outcome: Backend can enumerate all projects and runs, reading status and stage data from filesystem.
scope: Recursive scan of ~/odinclaw/portfolio/projects/
Parse state.yaml per project (runs list, status, current_stage)
Parse execution-log.json per run (stages, agent, model, duration, premiumRequests, tokens)
Return structured ProjectRun objects
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/projects returns all projects with their runs
Each run includes parsed stage data from execution-log.json
Missing or malformed files are handled gracefully (skipped with warning)
Response conforms to shared API types contract
assumptions: state.yaml and execution-log.json exist at documented paths
execution-log.json uses the schema observed in decimal-jump sample
items.split_rationale: Filesystem scan is independent of artifact reading.

items.title: Artifact reader: serve stage markdown artifacts as API responses
items.outcome: Frontend can fetch rendered artifact markdown for any stage of any run.
scope: GET /api/projects/:project/runs/:run/artifacts/:stage endpoint
Read numbered artifact files (00- through 80-) from run directory
Return raw markdown string
acceptance_criteria: Endpoint returns correct artifact content for valid stage
Returns 404 with clear message if artifact not found
Supports all numbered artifact file conventions
assumptions: Artifact files follow NN-name.md naming convention

items.title: Agent config reader: parse odinclaw.config.yaml for agent model assignments and team structure
items.outcome: API can serve agent metadata (model, team membership, role) to frontend.
scope: Parse odinclaw.config.yaml on startup
Derive Design team (Loki's agents) and Build team (Thor's agents) from config
GET /api/agents endpoint returning agent list with model, team, status
acceptance_criteria: All agents from config are returned with correct model assignments
Agents are grouped into design_team and build_team
Agent status (idle/working/blocked) is derived from active runs
assumptions: Config structure is stable; agents section contains model/provider fields

items.title: Limits snapshot: serve API/Copilot rate limit data from local snapshot file
items.outcome: Frontend can display remaining API limits without live external polling.
scope: Read limits snapshot file (e.g. data/limits-snapshot.json)
GET /api/limits endpoint
Schema: claudeRemaining, claudeResetAt, copilotRemaining, copilotResetAt, updatedAt
non_goals: Writing limits data from this endpoint
acceptance_criteria: Endpoint returns limits data if snapshot exists
Returns defaults/null values if snapshot missing
Includes updatedAt timestamp so frontend can show staleness
assumptions: Limits snapshot is written by a separate process or manually; this API only reads it

items.title: Zustand store: polling loop and global state slices for pipeline data
items.outcome: All views share a single reactive data store refreshed every 10 seconds.
scope: Zustand store with slices: projects, agents, limits
10-second polling interval with AbortController cleanup
Loading and error states per slice
Derived selectors: activeJobs, backlogJobs, completedJobs, agentStatus
acceptance_criteria: Store refreshes every 10 seconds
All views subscribe to shared store without their own fetches
Polling stops cleanly on unmount
Stale data indicator visible when last refresh > 30s ago
assumptions: 10-second polling is acceptable latency for pipeline monitoring

items.title: Dashboard Home: active job hero card with real-time stage indicator
items.outcome: User sees the most recent active job prominently with its current stage and agent.
scope: HeroCard component showing job title, issue number, project
Current stage indicator (stage name, agent avatar, duration)
Pulsing glow animation when actively processing
Links to full job detail
acceptance_criteria: Most recently updated active job is shown
Current stage and active agent avatar are visible
Pulsing animation is present when status=in_progress
If no active jobs, shows empty state
assumptions: Active job is determined by most recent run with status=in_progress

items.title: Dashboard Home: Design and Build agent team panels
items.outcome: User can see all agents in both teams with their current status at a glance.
scope: Two-column panel layout: Design Team | Build Team
AgentCard per agent: avatar, name, model badge, status pill (idle/working/blocked)
Active job name shown when agent is working
Mobile: horizontal scroll within each panel
non_goals: Clicking agent to drill into agent history
acceptance_criteria: Both teams visible side-by-side on desktop
Agent status derived correctly from active run stages
Model badge shows configured model (e.g. sonnet-4.6)
Mobile shows panels stacked with horizontal agent scroll
assumptions: Agent status idle/working/blocked is derivable from execution-log active stage

items.title: Dashboard Home: pipeline flow bar and cost summary header
items.outcome: User sees high-level pipeline throughput and cost/limits at all times.
scope: Pipeline flow bar: Backlog → Design Phase → Build Phase → Complete with job counts
Sticky cost/limits header: premium requests today/week, Claude remaining, Copilot remaining, estimated cost
Progress bars for limit usage
acceptance_criteria: Job counts in each pipeline phase are accurate
Cost header shows estimated total cost to date
Limit bars show % used vs total
Header is sticky/visible on scroll
assumptions: Premium request cost is $0.10 each

items.title: Backlog view: scrollable queued jobs list with expandable markdown
items.outcome: User can browse all queued jobs and read their full request markdown inline.
scope: Filter runs where status=backlog
Card list: title, issue number, date created, project
Click to expand inline rendered markdown (react-markdown)
Pull-to-refresh gesture on mobile
acceptance_criteria: All backlog jobs are listed in reverse-chronological order
Expanding a card shows rendered 00-request.md
Pull-to-refresh triggers store re-poll on mobile
Empty state shown when backlog is empty
assumptions: Backlog status is determined by state.yaml status field

items.title: Pipeline view: Design Phase horizontal stage flow with job cards
items.outcome: User can see all active design-phase jobs positioned at their current stage.
scope: Horizontal scrollable stage flow: Architecture → UX Design → System Map → Capability Slicing → Discovery → Issues → Issue Refinement → Planning QA
Job cards at each stage: title, issue#, project, agent avatar, duration, running cost
Pulsing glow on actively-processing cards
Animated card transition between stages on data refresh
acceptance_criteria: All design-phase active runs appear at correct stage
Stage flow is horizontally scrollable on mobile
Active cards pulse visually
Cost shows sum of premiumRequests × $0.10 so far
assumptions: Current stage is derivable from execution-log.json last completed entry

items.title: Pipeline view: Build Phase horizontal stage flow with job cards
items.outcome: User can see all active build-phase jobs positioned at their current stage.
scope: Horizontal scrollable stage flow: Foundation → Implementation → Code Review → Adversarial Testing → Integration Testing → Resilience Testing → Release → Recovery
Same job card anatomy as design phase
Separate section below design phase with clear visual separator
non_goals: Merging design and build flows into one
acceptance_criteria: All build-phase active runs appear at correct stage
Section is visually distinct from design phase
Empty state shown when no active build jobs
assumptions: Build phase stages are determined by execution-log stage names matching build team agents

items.title: Pipeline view: animated stage transition system with Framer Motion
items.outcome: Job cards animate smoothly when transitioning between stages on poll refresh.
scope: Framer Motion layoutId for shared card identity across renders
400ms ease transition
Respects prefers-reduced-motion
No layout shift outside the pipeline flow
acceptance_criteria: Card slides from old stage column to new stage column on refresh
Animation is disabled when OS reduces motion
No visual artifacts or position jumps during transition
assumptions: Framer Motion layoutId approach is used per UX spec

items.title: Completed view: jobs list with summary metrics and success/fail badges
items.outcome: User can browse all finished jobs with high-level cost and duration metrics.
scope: Filter runs where status=complete or status=failed
Card: title, project, completion date, total duration, total cost, total tokens, success/fail badge
Sort by completion date descending
acceptance_criteria: All completed/failed runs are listed
Total cost = sum(premiumRequests × $0.10) across all stages
Total duration = sum of stage durations
Success/fail badge matches run status
assumptions: Duration data is available in execution-log.json per stage

items.title: Completed view: full job detail overlay with two-column stage breakdown
items.outcome: User can drill into any completed job and see full stage-by-stage breakdown with rendered artifacts.
scope: Full-screen overlay/modal on card click
Two columns: Design stages (left) | Build stages (right)
Each stage: expandable accordion with agent avatar, model, duration, premiumRequests, cost, tokens
Rendered artifact markdown (react-markdown + syntax highlighting)
Summary row: totals
acceptance_criteria: Overlay opens on card click with close button
Both columns populated correctly from execution-log.json
Accordion sections expand to show artifact markdown
Summary row shows correct totals
Mobile: tabs instead of columns
assumptions: Artifact markdown is fetchable via BC-01-C2 endpoint
items.split_rationale: Overlay is complex enough to be separate from the list

items.title: Analytics: cost dashboard with time-series and breakdown charts
items.outcome: User can understand cost trends over time and which agents/projects drive spending.
scope: Daily/weekly/monthly premium request consumption chart (recharts LineChart/BarChart)
Cost per project bar chart
Cost per agent bar chart
Cost per model tier (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) breakdown
acceptance_criteria: Charts render correctly with real execution-log data
Time granularity can be switched (daily/weekly/monthly)
Empty state shown if no data available
assumptions: Timestamps in execution-log.json are ISO8601

items.title: Analytics: performance dashboard (agent stage durations, success rates)
items.outcome: User can identify slow agents, failure patterns, and Design vs Build phase timing.
scope: Average stage duration per agent (bar chart)
Average job completion time (line/area chart)
Success/failure/retry rates per agent
Design phase vs Build phase duration comparison
non_goals: Individual run drill-down from analytics (use Completed view)
acceptance_criteria: All charts populated from execution-log data
Retry rate shown where retries field is present
Design vs Build comparison visible as side-by-side bars
assumptions: execution-log.json may not have retries field — show N/A if absent

items.title: Analytics: limits tracker with usage bars and burn rate projection
items.outcome: User can see remaining API limits and whether they will run out before reset.
scope: Claude API: remaining requests, reset time, % used progress bar
GitHub Copilot: remaining premium requests, reset time, % used progress bar
Burn rate: requests/day based on recent history, projected days to limit
Visual warning when projected to exceed limit before reset
non_goals: Live external API polling (reads snapshot file)
acceptance_criteria: Both limit sources shown with progress bars
Reset time shown in human-readable format
Burn rate projection shows 'on track' or 'at risk'
Shows staleness indicator if snapshot is > 1 hour old
assumptions: Burn rate calculated from execution-log timestamps over rolling 7-day window

items.title: Design system: shared component library (tokens, AgentCard, JobCard, StageChip, CostBar)
items.outcome: All views built from consistent, reusable components with the Valhalla dark theme.
scope: Tailwind config: dark theme tokens (colors, spacing, glow effects)
AgentCard: avatar, name, model badge, status pill
JobCard: title, issue#, project, agent, duration, cost, pulse animation
StageChip: stage name, status indicator
CostBar: progress bar with labels
AgentAvatar: maps agent name to correct image from public/img/agents/
non_goals: View-level layout components (belong in respective view slices)
acceptance_criteria: All components render in Storybook or equivalent isolated view
AgentAvatar correctly maps all 21 agents to their image files
Dark theme applied consistently
Pulsing glow animation works on JobCard
All components are fully typed
assumptions: Avatar files exist in public/img/agents/ as confirmed in UX spec; ratatoskr file has known typo ratatoksr.PNG

items.title: Application shell: routing, navigation, and mobile tab layout
items.outcome: Users can navigate between all 5 views via desktop sidebar and mobile tab bar.
scope: React Router (or Vite-native) with routes: /, /backlog, /pipeline, /completed, /analytics
Desktop: left sidebar navigation with icons
Mobile: bottom tab bar
Active route highlighted
Page transitions (fade)
non_goals: Deep-linking to specific jobs (can be added later)
acceptance_criteria: All 5 routes resolve to correct view components
Nav highlights active route
Mobile tab bar is visible and functional
Page transition is smooth on navigation
assumptions: No authentication required for MVP

items.title: API–Frontend contract: shared TypeScript types and OpenAPI-style schema
items.outcome: Frontend and backend share a single source of truth for all API response shapes.
scope: src/types/api.ts defining: Project, Run, Stage, Agent, LimitsSnapshot, CostSummary
Backend serialises responses to match these types
Frontend normaliser validates/transforms API responses to store types
non_goals: Runtime validation library (manual types are sufficient for MVP)
acceptance_criteria: All API response shapes defined in shared types
Backend and frontend import from same types file
No shape mismatches between API response and frontend store
assumptions: Monorepo or co-located frontend/backend so types can be shared directly

items.title: Discovery: confirm state.yaml schema, execution-log.json schema, and stage-to-phase mapping
items.outcome: All data model unknowns are resolved before BC-01 and INT-01 implementation begins.
scope: Enumerate all status values used in state.yaml across existing projects
Confirm execution-log.json schema: field names, nullability, stage name conventions
Determine how design phases vs build phases are identified from stage names
Confirm premiumRequests field: always present, fractional values, null handling
Check if 40-build/ directory convention is used or if artifacts are always single files
acceptance_criteria: Documented enumeration of all status values
Annotated execution-log.json schema with field types and nullability
Decision on design/build phase derivation strategy
Confirmation of premiumRequests value semantics
